what: INVEST score for FSC-2417: 77/100 (PASS_WITH_CONCERNS) — I=14 N=10 V=20 E=18 S=11 T=20
why: INVEST Quality Agent scored the story across six readiness dimensions. A normalised score < 80 triggers a Gate G1 block. Found 0 blocking issue(s) and 0 improvement suggestion(s).

dimension_rationales.independent: The story is largely self-contained with well-defined FSC objects and components, but it implicitly depends on the existing MiFID II risk profile data, objectives register, and financial circumstances records being present and correctly structured — these upstream data dependencies are not tracked as separate stories yet could block implementation or testing.
dimension_rationales.negotiable: The user goal and regulatory outcome are clearly stated, but the Technical Scope section over-specifies implementation detail — naming the exact Apex class (SuitabilityScoreCalculator), LWC component (suitability-dashboard), custom field API name and data type (Number 3,2), and Platform Event name (SuitabilityScoreUpdated__e) — leaving the development team little room to negotiate the technical approach.
dimension_rationales.valuable: The story directly and explicitly addresses COBS 9.2 suitability compliance and Consumer Duty PS22/9 good outcomes, eliminating a manual, error-prone cross-system process; the regulatory and adviser-efficiency value is unambiguous and substantial.
dimension_rationales.estimable: All FSC objects are named (Suitability__c, FinancialAccount, RiskProfile__c), the Apex trigger pattern, LWC, custom field, and Platform Event are identified, scope is bounded with a clear Out-of-Scope section, and four fully-formed ACs provide complexity anchors — a team could estimate this confidently in story points, with only minor ambiguity around the weighting algorithm logic.
dimension_rationales.small: The story touches multiple FSC objects (Suitability__c, FinancialAccount, RiskProfile__c), introduces a new Apex class, a new LWC, a custom field, and a Platform Event with real-time refresh, plus four distinct AC scenarios including a compliance warning flow — this is at the upper boundary of a single sprint and carries meaningful delivery risk.
dimension_rationales.testable: All four acceptance criteria are written in full Given/When/Then format covering the happy path (Scenario 1), an automated recalculation trigger (Scenario 2), a stale-data edge case (Scenario 3), and a regulatory compliance warning with audit trail (Scenario 4) — comprehensive coverage of happy path, error state, edge case, and compliance enforcement.
